$comment: Upstream parity ratchet for the eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort port. Fixtures under test/fixtures/*.json are captured verbatim from upstream by `pnpm run port:tests:simple-import-sort`; this file controls how strictly upstream.test.mjs replays them so parity can be ratcheted up rule-by-rule without silently dropping coverage. Per-rule `level`: `off` quarantines the rule (cases counted, none asserted), `valid` asserts only that upstream-valid code produces no diagnostics, `count` additionally asserts the diagnostic count on invalid cases, `full` additionally asserts the applied autofix output matches upstream exactly. `skipParsers` lists parser kinds (`flow`/`ts`/`js`) whose cases are counted and logged but not asserted — Oxc does not parse Flow syntax. Cases carrying an `eslint-disable` directive are always skipped (the linter core, not the rule, suppresses those). Every skipped/quarantined case is reported in the suite's parity summary, so coverage is never silently truncated. This port begins at `off`; follow-up PRs reimplement the upstream algorithm in Rust and ratchet each rule up to `full`.
